'''Thilis-Brin''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]] serving with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. A [[Technician/Legends|technician]], she was assigned to the [[Mon Calamari Orbital Shipyards|Mon Calamari Shipyards]] as a troubleshooter after completing recruit training. After two [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Thilis-Brin desired a more active Alliance posting and accepted an offer to join [[Shroud Team]]—an all-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] unit of [[Foster Agent]]s tasked with returning stranded operatives to the Alliance—under the command of [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Dutra Zeneta]], a member of the [[Jin]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Based out of [[Corint City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pirik]], she served as the group's technician and secondary Foster Agent after the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Dheendo]]. A thrill-seeker, Thilis-Brin reveled in using her personal [[Sunwolf speeder bike]] to assist in operative extractions while evading Imperial pursuit, such as the [[Time/Legends|time]] she successfully helped a high-[[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] [[Unidentified Alliance officer (Pirik)|officer]] return to the Alliance after a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] chase through the [[Talamp Industrial Sector]].

==Biography==
Thilis-Brin was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]]<ref name="Shroud" /> [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] sometime between [[36 BBY/Legends|36 BBY]] and [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="T-age" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], she joined the insurgent [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and underwent recruit training, placing first in her class. She also placed fourth in the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] training seminar, an impressive achievement given the [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] physiology of the Ishi Tib. Upon graduation from her training, Thilis-Brin was regarded as [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|SpecForce]] material, but she was assigned to the [[Mon Calamari Orbital Shipyards]], working as a [[Technician/Legends|technical troubleshooter]]. However, she longed to take a more active role in the Alliance. Two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after enlisting, Thilis-Brin received the opportunity she desired. She was selected by the [[Jin]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Dutra Zeneta]] to serve as part of [[Shroud Team]], an all-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] unit of [[Foster Agent]]s.<ref name="Shroud" /> A posting as a Foster Agent was one of the most dangerous assignments in the Alliance; they were the point of contact for [[operative]]s stranded in the field who were attempting to rejoin the Alliance. The names and locations of the Foster Agents were provided to those operatives who may have need of their services; if the operatives were captured and interrogated, the Foster Agent was in danger of being compromised.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref>

Shroud Team was inserted into [[Corint City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pirik]] in the [[Divis Arm/Legends|Divis Arm]]; in addition to Thilis-Brin and Zeneta, the team also consisted of the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Dheendo]], the [[Carosite]] [[Tamo Lan]], and the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Gondara]]. The unit's composition blended well with the inhabitants of Corint [[City/Legends|City]], as non-[[Human/Legends|Humans]] made up 95 percent of the local population. Thilis-Brin served as the team's chief technician and [[Quartermaster/Legends|procurement specialist]], who would obtain needed supplies, such as vehicles and [[Ration pack/Legends|rations]]. She was also Shroud Team's secondary Foster Agent, with Dheendo operating as the primary. However, her Ishi Tib physiology meant that she needed to immerse herself in a [[Salt/Legends|brine]] solution every [[Standard day/Legends|day]]. The closest available source to Corint City was the [[Uloitir Sea]], located fourteen [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] from the settlement, and Thilis-Brin had to find the necessary [[Time/Legends|time]] in the day to attend to her physiological needs while also performing her duties with Shroud Team. At some point during her assignment on Pirik, Thilis-Brin was involved in a high-speed [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] chase through the [[Talamp Industrial Sector]]. Riding her modified [[Ikas-Adno/Legends|Ikas-Adno]] [[Sunwolf speeder bike]], she evaded a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout troopers]] while transporting a high-[[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] [[Unidentified Alliance officer (Pirik)|Alliance officer]]. Evading pursuit and positive identification, she ensured her charge was safely delivered to their destination.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Personality and traits==
Thilis-Brin was an individual driven to succeed, be it on a personal level or in her goal to assist in the destruction of the Empire. Her scores during her Alliance training marked her as an operative with a promising career, which Thilis-Brin began to live up to once she started working with Shroud Team. Although she enjoyed her work with the unit, Thilis-Brin was unsure of exactly what kind of career she wanted to pursue with the Alliance and in what capacity she wanted to serve in the future. While Ishi Tib were generally known to be mild-mannered individuals, Thilis-Brin was not. She was a show-off who lived for thrills and was noted for rescuing Alliance operatives in a high-profile manner.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Skills and abilities==
Thilis-Brin was qualified to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] both [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]], and she was rated to handle the weapon and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]] systems aboard the former. She was also capable of [[Astrogation/Legends|navigating]] [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and using [[Comm unit/Legends|communications]] and security equipment. A skilled technician, Thilis-Brin was trained to repair capital ships, with particular expertise with [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigates]] and [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruisers]], and space transports, as well as maintaining their weapon systems. She was also able to [[Programmer/Legends|program]] and repair both [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and [[Computer/Legends|computers]].<ref name="Shroud" />

Thilis-Brin's assignment to the Calamari [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] gave her an insight into the [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]] needed to keep the installation functioning efficiently, as well as knowledge regarding [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari's]] [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement]] procedures. She was also well-informed about business protocols, item valuation, and Corint City itself. She was skilled in the operation of [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]]-equipped vehicles and could repair them when the need arose. Trained in survival techniques, Thilis-Brin placed fourth in desert survival during her training. A member of the aquatic Ishi Tib species, Thilis-Brin was a natural and strong swimmer, although her need to be immersed in salt water on a daily basis was an occasional hindrance, as she would have to find the time to do so while also operating as a member of the Alliance. In addition to that species trait, Thilis-Brin also possessed a hardened beak capable of inflicting harm during [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]], and she was a natural planner like the majority of her race. She was trained in the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and could also operate vehicle-mounted blasters.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Equipment==
Thilis-Brin generally armed herself with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and tool kit. She also owned a Sunwolf speeder bike, which was the military variant of the Ikas-Adno's civilian-model [[Starhawk speeder bike/Legends|Starhawk]].<ref name="Shroud" />

==Behind the scenes==
Thilis-Brin first appeared in an installment of the "[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)|Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]" article series published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 10]] by [[West End Games]] in [[1996]]. Detailing the members of Shroud Team, the feature was written by [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]] with illustrations provided by [[Kathy Burdette]]. Thilis-Brin was later namechecked as an example name for Ishi Tib characters in the ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', published in [[2003]], and ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'', released in [[2010]], both from [[Wizards of the Coast]].
